rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Use component type annexes for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Use implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type annexes when concrete bounds are available
Write output dependency guarantees in component type annexes that relate visible outputs to visible inputs
generic_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks in the appropriate scope to maintain architectural integrity
Use visible identifiers from the AADL model to ensure consistency
Avoid using subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
Use comparison expressions for numeric ranges and dependencies
anti_patterns: Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
